title: Nginx Docker image pulled is outdated
body:
### Issue Description

Podman pulls an outdated version of `nginx:mainline-alpine-slim` from Docker Hub.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Run `podman pull docker.io/library/nginx:mainline-alpine-slim`
2. Run `podman image ls`

### Describe the results you received

The creation date of the pulled container image says "3 weeks ago".

### Describe the results you expected

The creation date of the pulled image container image should be  [9 days ago](https://hub.docker.com/_/nginx/tags?page=1&name=mainline-alpine-slim) (as of today) instead of 3 weeks ago.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Same error on a different Linux system running AlmaLinux 8 and Podman 4.2.0.

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
Thank you for reaching out, @MatsG23 !

The image's create timestamp states `"Created": "2023-02-11T10:04:00.793725955Z"` and Podman today correctly reports the image to be created "4 weeks ago". 

> The creation date of the pulled image container image should be [9 days ago](https://hub.docker.com/_/nginx/tags?page=1&name=mainline-alpine-slim) (as of today) instead of 3 weeks ago.

Note that the link to Docker Hub refers to the "last push".  That does not imply the build date of an image.

So, long story short: everything seems in order.
